title: Getting started
text: cce keeps one small output contract across two workflows: observed two-arm comparison fit_cce_vs() SOC-projection project_soc_only()
code: demo <- cce_demo_data(n = 200, seed = 1) head(demo) #>   arm time event       subgroup age ps #> 1 SOC   83     1 Biomarker-high  69  2 #> 2   A  214     0     All-comers  53  1 #> 3 SOC  123     1 Biomarker-high  74  0 #> 4 SOC  405     0 Biomarker-high  64  1 #> 5   A  341     0 Biomarker-high  70  1 #> 6 SOC  280     1  Biomarker-low  73  0

title: Getting started
code: vs_fit <- fit_cce_vs(   data = demo,   arm = "arm",   time = "time",   event = "event",   subgroup = "subgroup",   tau = 365,   times = c(180, 365) )  vs_fit #> cce_fit #>   mode: vs  #>   label: Observed comparison  #>   curves: 722 rows #>   effects: 12 rows #>   diagnostics: 16 rows head(as_effects_df(vs_fit)) #>   mode   subgroup contrast         metric time    estimate #> 1   vs        All  A - SOC delta_survival  180   0.2915253 #> 2   vs        All  A - SOC delta_survival  365   0.1938709 #> 3   vs        All  A - SOC     delta_rmst  365  99.2640724 #> 4   vs All-comers  A - SOC delta_survival  180   0.3017719 #> 5   vs All-comers  A - SOC delta_survival  365   0.2331357 #> 6   vs All-comers  A - SOC     delta_rmst  365 102.4089881

title: Getting started
code: soc_fit <- project_soc_only(   data = demo,   arm = "arm",   time = "time",   event = "event",   subgroup = "subgroup",   tau = 365,   hr_scenarios = c(0.7, 0.85, 1.0),   target_delta_rmst = 30 )  soc_fit #> cce_fit #>   mode: soc_only  #>   label: Projection (assumption-based)  #>   curves: 764 rows #>   effects: 16 rows #>   diagnostics: 8 rows head(as_effects_df(soc_fit)) #>       mode   subgroup          contrast      metric time  estimate #> 1 soc_only        All     HR_0.70 - SOC  delta_rmst  365 55.889011 #> 2 soc_only        All     HR_0.85 - SOC  delta_rmst  365 25.771121 #> 3 soc_only        All     HR_1.00 - SOC  delta_rmst  365  0.000000 #> 4 soc_only        All required_hr - SOC required_hr  365  0.827455 #> 5 soc_only All-comers     HR_0.70 - SOC  delta_rmst  365 51.605992 #> 6 soc_only All-comers     HR_0.85 - SOC  delta_rmst  365 23.531579

title: Getting started
text: package always writes four files: curves.csv effects.csv diagnostics.csv results.json
code: out_dir <- tempfile("cce-site-") write_cce_results(vs_fit, out_dir) list.files(out_dir) #> [1] "curves.csv"      "diagnostics.csv" "effects.csv"     "results.json"

title: Design guide
text: package rebuilt minimize size disk dependency surface. design rules : use source files main keep public functions explicit avoid large bundled data avoid external tutorial downloads make pkgdown reproducible source

title: Design guide
text: workflows return components: curves effects diagnostics metadata common contract matters algorithmic breadth. package optimized lightweight reuse.

title: Design guide
text: package separates two use cases: fit_cce_vs() observed versus SOC project_soc_only() assumption-based planning SOC keeps implementation simple still covering main planning questions addressed package.

title: Design guide
text: Tutorials use : synthetic data cce_demo_data() survival::veteran, already distributed survival large external data downloaded package documentation.

title: Tutorial: demo data
code: demo <- cce_demo_data(n = 240, seed = 10) table(demo$arm, demo$subgroup) #>       #>       All-comers Biomarker-high Biomarker-low #>   A           41             45            33 #>   SOC         43             38            40

title: Tutorial: demo data
code: vs_fit <- fit_cce_vs(   data = demo,   subgroup = "subgroup",   tau = 365,   times = c(180, 365) )  head(as_curves_df(vs_fit)) #>   mode subgroup method arm time  survival n_risk #> 1   vs      All     km   A    0 1.0000000    119 #> 2   vs      All     km   A    2 1.0000000    119 #> 3   vs      All     km   A    4 0.9915966    119 #> 4   vs      All     km   A    9 0.9831933    118 #> 5   vs      All     km   A   12 0.9663866    117 #> 6   vs      All     km   A   16 0.9579832    115 head(as_diagnostics_df(vs_fit)) #>   mode   subgroup arm metric value #> 1   vs        All   A      n   119 #> 2   vs        All   A events    59 #> 3   vs        All SOC      n   121 #> 4   vs        All SOC events    78 #> 5   vs All-comers   A      n    41 #> 6   vs All-comers   A events    24

title: Tutorial: demo data
code: soc_fit <- project_soc_only(   data = demo,   subgroup = "subgroup",   tau = 365,   hr_scenarios = c(0.65, 0.80, 1.00),   target_delta_rmst = 25 )  head(as_effects_df(soc_fit)) #>       mode   subgroup          contrast      metric time   estimate #> 1 soc_only        All     HR_0.65 - SOC  delta_rmst  365 65.9071438 #> 2 soc_only        All     HR_0.80 - SOC  delta_rmst  365 34.6511694 #> 3 soc_only        All     HR_1.00 - SOC  delta_rmst  365  0.0000000 #> 4 soc_only        All required_hr - SOC required_hr  365  0.8516335 #> 5 soc_only All-comers     HR_0.65 - SOC  delta_rmst  365 49.1891369 #> 6 soc_only All-comers     HR_0.80 - SOC  delta_rmst  365 25.8445192

title: Tutorial: demo data
code: out_dir <- tempfile("cce-demo-") write_cce_results(soc_fit, out_dir) list.files(out_dir) #> [1] "curves.csv"      "diagnostics.csv" "effects.csv"     "results.json"

title: Tutorial: public survival data
text: survival::veteran public dataset already distributed survival package, tutorial needs external download.
code: data(veteran, package = "survival") #> Warning in data(veteran, package = "survival"): data set 'veteran' not found  veteran2 <- transform(   veteran,   arm = ifelse(trt == 1, "SOC", "A"),   time = time,   event = status,   subgroup = as.character(celltype) )  head(veteran2[, c("arm", "time", "event", "subgroup")]) #>   arm time event subgroup #> 1 SOC   72     1 squamous #> 2 SOC  411     1 squamous #> 3 SOC  228     1 squamous #> 4 SOC  126     1 squamous #> 5 SOC  118     1 squamous #> 6 SOC   10     1 squamous

title: Tutorial: public survival data
code: vs_fit <- fit_cce_vs(   data = veteran2,   subgroup = "subgroup",   tau = 180,   times = c(90, 180) )  head(as_effects_df(vs_fit)) #>   mode subgroup contrast         metric time     estimate #> 1   vs      All  A - SOC delta_survival   90  -0.16657817 #> 2   vs      All  A - SOC delta_survival  180   0.02042615 #> 3   vs      All  A - SOC     delta_rmst  180  18.13311506 #> 4   vs    adeno  A - SOC delta_survival   90  -0.41666667 #> 5   vs    adeno  A - SOC delta_survival  180   0.06944444 #> 6   vs    adeno  A - SOC     delta_rmst  180 -10.83333333

title: Tutorial: public survival data
code: soc_fit <- project_soc_only(   data = veteran2,   subgroup = "subgroup",   tau = 180,   hr_scenarios = c(0.75, 0.90, 1.00),   target_delta_rmst = 10 )  head(as_effects_df(soc_fit)) #>       mode subgroup          contrast      metric time   estimate #> 1 soc_only      All     HR_0.75 - SOC  delta_rmst  180 37.0727933 #> 2 soc_only      All     HR_0.90 - SOC  delta_rmst  180 12.8114755 #> 3 soc_only      All     HR_1.00 - SOC  delta_rmst  180  0.0000000 #> 4 soc_only      All required_hr - SOC required_hr  180  0.9204667 #> 5 soc_only    adeno     HR_0.75 - SOC  delta_rmst  180 13.5622187 #> 6 soc_only    adeno     HR_0.90 - SOC  delta_rmst  180  5.0077686

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dai. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dai (2026). cce: Counterfactual Comparator Workflows Survival Data. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/dai540/cce.
code: @Manual{,   title = {cce: Counterfactual Comparator Workflows for Survival Data},   author = {{Dai}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/dai540/cce}, }

title: cce
text: cce small R package counterfactual comparator work survival settings. package intentionally narrow. try general causal inference framework. Instead, provides minimal consistent set workflows two concrete jobs: observed two-arm comparison workflow versus SOC SOC-projection workflow assumption-based scenario planning package built stay small: bundled large datasets generated website files committed main heavy downstream dependencies beyond survival jsonlite large example outputs cached artifacts

title: cce
text: cce focuses single output contract simple survival analysis. workflows return: survival curves effect tables diagnostics tables metadata payload can exported JSON contract designed lightweight benchmarking, early planning, tutorial-friendly analyses analyst needs consistent set outputs without bringing large framework.

title: cce
text: package expects patient-level analysis table. minimal columns : arm: treatment label time: follow-time event: event indicator Optional columns can added without changing workflow: subgroup: subgroup label stratified summaries baseline columns age ps tutorials, package supplies: cce_demo_data() synthetic demo data public-data tutorial based survival::veteran, ships inside survival require external download

title: cce
text: package keeps public API deliberately small: cce_demo_data(): synthetic survival dataset examples tests fit_cce_vs(): observed two-arm survival comparison project_soc_only(): SOC-proportional-hazards projection as_curves_df(): extract curves table as_effects_df(): extract effects table as_diagnostics_df(): extract diagnostics table write_cce_results(): export results CSV JSON

title: cce
text: fit_cce_vs() smallest useful observed-comparison workflow package. : enforces two-arm input computes Kaplan-Meier style survival curves reports survival differences requested time points reports restricted mean survival time differences tau returns compact diagnostics sample counts event counts

title: cce
text: project_soc_only() starts observed SOC curve projects new curves using proportional-hazards assumption. : estimates observed SOC survival curve creates projected curves user-supplied hazard-ratio scenarios reports projected survival differences projected RMST gains can optionally back-solve required_hr target RMST gain outputs explicitly labeled projection-based read causal estimates.

title: cce
text: write_cce_results() writes minimal export set: curves.csv effects.csv diagnostics.csv results.json export schema intentionally simple long-form, can consumed spreadsheets, reports, small downstream scripts without extra adapters.

title: cce
text: repository designed used source checkout. run tests locally: build documentation site locally:
code: install.packages(c("pkgload", "testthat", "pkgdown", "rmarkdown")) pkgload::load_all("path/to/cce", export_all = FALSE) testthat::test_local("path/to/cce") pkgdown::build_site("path/to/cce", install = FALSE, new_process = FALSE)

title: cce
code: demo <- cce::cce_demo_data(n = 200, seed = 1)  vs_fit <- cce::fit_cce_vs(   data = demo,   arm = "arm",   time = "time",   event = "event",   subgroup = "subgroup",   tau = 365,   times = c(180, 365) )  head(cce::as_effects_df(vs_fit))  soc_fit <- cce::project_soc_only(   data = demo,   arm = "arm",   time = "time",   event = "event",   subgroup = "subgroup",   tau = 365,   hr_scenarios = c(0.7, 0.85, 1.0),   target_delta_rmst = 30 )  head(cce::as_effects_df(soc_fit))

title: cce
text: pkgdown site organized four sections: Getting Started: first-pass walkthrough package Guides: design output-contract guidance Tutorials: end--end analyses demo data public data Reference: function reference generated package docs

title: cce
text: rebuild cce follows strict small-package rule set: source files tracked main large generated artifacts excluded tutorial data must either synthetic come already-installed packages package keeps code file structure intentionally shallow

title: cce
text: repository uses smallest practical R package layout: R/: package code man/: reference files tests/: testthat tests vignettes/: pkgdown articles .github/workflows/: test pkgdown automation

title: cce
text: cce intentionally limited. implement: time-varying treatment strategies high-dimensional covariate adjustment automatic propensity-score modeling external data downloads tutorials heavy reporting frameworks inside package omissions deliberate. package kept small disk small dependency surface.

title: cce
text: Website: https://dai540.github.io/cce/

title: Fit an observed two-arm survival comparison — fit_cce_vs
text: Fits simple observed survival comparison returns curves, effect tables, diagnostics, metadata shared output contract.

title: Fit an observed two-arm survival comparison — fit_cce_vs
code: fit_cce_vs(   data,   arm = "arm",   time = "time",   event = "event",   subgroup = NULL,   tau,   times = tau )

title: Fit an observed two-arm survival comparison — fit_cce_vs
text: data data frame. arm Arm column name. time Follow-time column name. event Event indicator column name. subgroup Optional subgroup column name. tau Horizon RMST calculation. times Time points used survival differences.

title: Project SOC-only survival scenarios — project_soc_only
text: data data frame. arm Arm column name. time Follow-time column name. event Event indicator column name. subgroup Optional subgroup column name. soc_level Label used standard care. tau Horizon RMST calculation. hr_scenarios Numeric vector hazard-ratio scenarios. target_delta_rmst Optional target RMST gain used back-solve required hazard ratio.
